content:
built a website for a client at the agency i work for minimum-code, designed first in figma then backend and logic built after design
what I see is 2 types, AI made websites that look vibecoded in an afternoon and full custom builds that take months, i don't think both work well now
I use AI tools to build faster, but I still plan the logic myself, AI doesnt know your business, I still decide that before building anything
plan the database and the logic before the frontend, skipping this leads to a huge mess and rebuilds, plus AI doesnt understand your intentions (edit backend to fit frontend or visa versa)
change effort based on the need of the website, a landing page doesnt need the same backend work as a website with payment processing
test when AI finishes builds, not just ask AI to verify (even tho it helps in 50% cases), a simple signup form can work fine and still break when a customer tries a bunch of stuff with it
fast website development dont mean good website, choosing what data to save, how permissions work, what happens when a payment fails, that still takes time to think no matter how fast AI builds it
anyway AI is just a tool. it is bad in the hands of bad developers, and great in the hands of great developers
thoughts on using AI for backend specifically, not just UI?

  submitted by   /u/Jeanne_Nka
[link]   [comments]
